meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template Tutorial
content.seo_description: Master the Antigravity project template to overcome SaaS knowledge gaps. A high-efficiency guide for freelancers using AI-powered development workflows.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template bridges the SaaS knowledge gap by providing a pre-configured, production-ready architecture. By leveraging Claude Code to scaffold and iterate on this template, freelancers can bypass manual setup, enforce enterprise-grade security, and deploy scalable SaaS applications in hours rather than weeks, effectively scaling their development velocity.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re staring at a blank repository, paralyzed by the sheer complexity of modern SaaS architecture. You know how to code, but you don't know how to architect a scalable SaaS product from scratch. This is the 'no SaaS knowledge' trap—a silent killer of freelance careers.

But what if you didn't have to build the foundation? The Antigravity project template is your shortcut to high-end delivery. It’s not just boilerplate; it’s a battle-tested blueprint designed to handle authentication, database migrations, and CI/CD pipelines out of the box. Combine this with the raw intelligence of Claude Code, and you transform from a struggling freelancer into a high-velocity product engineer.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the base environment to ensure all subsequent code generation follows the project's strict architectural patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new Antigravity project with standard SaaS boilerplate, including Supabase auth and Stripe integration modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize Antigravity project with standard SaaS stack'

steps.title: Define Domain Models
steps.description: Map out your client's specific business logic into the template's schema to ensure data integrity and type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including Organizations, Users, and Subscription tiers.

steps.title: Implement API Endpoints
steps.description: Use the template's built-in API routing to create secure, authenticated endpoints without manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create CRUD API routes for the Project entity with role-based access control (RBAC) middleware enabled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate secure CRUD API routes with RBAC middleware'

steps.title: Configure Frontend Components
steps.description: Leverage Antigravity's component library to build responsive, accessible UI screens that match your client's brand.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dashboard view using the Antigravity UI component library, fetching data from the Project API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add src/components/Dashboard.tsx --prompt 'Build dashboard UI with API integration'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Execute the final deployment workflow to move your project from local development to a live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for Vercel, including environment variable setup and build script optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Optimize build pipeline and deploy to Vercel'

pro_tips: Always use the --dry-run flag in Claude Code to review generated files before they overwrite your existing architecture.
Force Antigravity to use absolute imports to keep your codebase clean as you add more complex features.
Treat the template as immutable; extend it via custom hooks rather than modifying core files to ensure easy upgrades.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature after every step to catch potential security vulnerabilities in authentication logic.
Save your most complex Antigravity prompts as local text files to create a 'prompt library' for future client projects.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, integrate a monitoring tool like Sentry and set up a feedback loop with your client to iterate on the features using the same Antigravity prompt workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a senior architect. Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
